 {"id":"bd-905b","title":"Define collision handling policy","description":"**Clear policy for path collision handling**

## What
Define what happens when collision is detected (different repos -> same path).

## Why
Current plan detects collisions but doesn't specify the behavior.

## Policy Options
1. **Warn and skip second repo** (safest, current implicit behavior)
2. **Fail fast** (strict, stops processing)
3. **Prompt user** (interactive only)
4. **Auto-rename with suffix** (risky)

## Chosen Policy: Warn and skip
```
Collision detected:
  Path: /home/user/projects/utils
  Configured: org1/utils (will be synced)
  Skipped:    org2/utils (same path)

To fix: Change layout to 'owner-repo' in config:
  ru config --set LAYOUT=owner-repo
```

## Implementation
- First occurrence wins
- Log warning with resolution hint
- Include in summary as 'skipped:collision'
- Exit code 2 (conflicts) if any collisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Clear messaging about collision
- First-wins policy
- Resolution hint in output
- Documented in README","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:11.250738889-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in ru script SECTION 9B","labels":["lists","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.858988726-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"bd-905b","depends_on_id":"bd-904","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:13.890572977-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9j92","title":"Implement main dashboard view (ntm mode)","description":"Task: Implement Main Dashboard View

Purpose
-------
Full-screen TUI dashboard showing all pending questions, active sessions,
and summary statistics in a organized layout.

Layout Design
-------------
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  ru review                                    Progress: 5/8  Runtime  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                       |
|  PENDING QUESTIONS (3)                                                |
|  -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
|  [1] ● project-alpha     Issue #42     Priority: CRITICAL             |
|      Context: Authentication failing on Windows                       |
|      > a) Quick fix  b) Full refactor  c) Skip                       |
|                                                                       |
|  [2] ○ project-beta      PR #15        Priority: NORMAL              |
|      [Press Enter to expand]                                          |
|                                                                       |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  ACTIVE SESSIONS                                                      |
|  -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
|  Repo              State         Progress      Health                 |
|  project-delta     GENERATING    80%           Healthy                |
|  project-epsilon   THINKING      60%           Healthy                |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  SUMMARY                                                              |
|  Completed: 4 | Issues: 7 | PRs: 1 | Commits: 12                     |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [1-9] Answer [Enter] Expand [d] Drill [s] Skip [a] Apply [q] Quit    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+

Implementation
--------------

Dashboard State
  declare -A DASHBOARD_STATE=(
    [selected_index]=0
    [expanded_question]=""
    [scroll_offset]=0
    [panel_focus]="questions"
  )

render_dashboard()
  clear
  render_header
  render_questions_panel
  render_sessions_panel
  render_summary_panel
  render_footer

render_questions_panel()
  Load questions from queue
  For each question:
    if expanded: show full context
    else: show one-line summary
  Highlight selected with ANSI

render_sessions_panel()
  Query session states
  Format as table with columns

render_summary_panel()
  Load metrics from state
  Format as single line stats

Dashboard Loop
--------------
run_dashboard()
  while true:
    render_dashboard
    read -rsn1 key
    handle_keypress "$key"
    
    # Check for new events
    poll_events

handle_keypress()
  case key:
    1-9: select_and_answer
    Enter: toggle_expand
    d: open_drilldown
    s: skip_current
    q: confirm_quit

Refresh Logic
-------------
- Full refresh every 5 seconds
- Immediate refresh on user action
- Poll for new questions/events

Terminal Handling
-----------------
- Save cursor position
- Use alternate screen buffer
- Restore on exit
- Handle resize (SIGWINCH)

Testing
-------
- Verify layout renders correctly
- Verify keyboard navigation
- Verify refresh updates data
- Verify resize handling
- Test with different terminal sizes

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Dashboard renders cleanly
- [ ] Questions panel shows all pending
- [ ] Sessions panel updates in real-time
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work
- [ ] Handles terminal resize","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:43.609323436-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:43.609323436-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9j92","depends_on_id":"bd-4ps0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:08:34.753044804-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-9s7y","title":"Implement local driver (tmux + stream-json)","description":"# Task: Implement Local Driver (tmux + stream-json)

## Purpose
Implement the session driver interface using tmux and Claude's stream-json output mode. This is the fallback driver when ntm is not available.

## Background
Claude Code supports --output-format stream-json which emits NDJSON events:
- System init with session_id and available tools
- Assistant messages with text and tool_use
- Tool results
- Final result with status

We capture this output and parse it for question detection.

## Implementation

### local_driver_start_session()
```bash
local_driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    
    # Create named pipe for event streaming
    rm -f "$event_pipe"
    mkfifo "$event_pipe"
    
    # Build claude command
    local claude_cmd="claude -p $(printf '%q' "$prompt") --output-format stream-json"
    
    # Create tmux session
    tmux new-session -d -s "$session_name" -c "$wt_path" \
        "exec $claude_cmd 2>&1 | tee '$log_file' > '$event_pipe'"
    
    # Return session info
    cat << EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_name",
  "worktree": "$wt_path",
  "log_file": "$log_file",
  "event_pipe": "$event_pipe",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_send_to_session()
```bash
local_driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
    
    # Send via tmux
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" "$message" Enter
    
    return $?
}
```

### local_driver_get_session_state()
```bash
local_driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Check if tmux session exists
    if ! tmux has-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo '{"state": "complete", "reason": "session_ended"}'
        return
    fi
    
    # Capture last lines of output
    local output
    output=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$session_id" -p -S -50 2>/dev/null)
    
    # Detect state from output
    local state="generating"
    local wait_reason=""
    
    # Check for prompt (waiting)
    if echo "$output" | grep -qE '^\s*>\s*$|Press Enter|Select.*:|Enter.*:'; then
        state="waiting"
        wait_reason="prompt_detected"
    fi
    
    # Check for errors
    if echo "$output" | grep -qiE 'error:|panic:|rate.limit|429'; then
        state="error"
    fi
    
    cat << EOF
{
  "state": "$state",
  "wait_reason": "$wait_reason",
  "session_id": "$session_id"
}
EOF
}
```

### local_driver_stream_events()
```bash
local_driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    local wt_path="$3"
    
    local event_pipe="$wt_path/.ru/events.pipe"
    local log_file="$wt_path/.ru/session.log"
    
    # Parse stream-json events
    while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        
        # Parse JSON event type
        local event_type
        event_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // empty' 2>/dev/null)
        
        case "$event_type" in
            system)
                local session_id_from_event
                session_id_from_event=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.session_id // empty')
                $callback "init" "$session_id_from_event"
                ;;
            assistant)
                # Check for AskUserQuestion tool_use
                local tool_name
                tool_name=$(echo "$line" | jq -r \
                    '.message.content[]? | select(.type=="tool_use") | .name // empty' 2>/dev/null)
                
                if [[ "$tool_name" == "AskUserQuestion" ]]; then
                    local question_data
                    question_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c \
                        '.message.content[] | select(.name=="AskUserQuestion") | .input')
                    $callback "question" "$question_data"
                fi
                ;;
            result)
                local status
                status=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.status // "unknown"')
                $callback "complete" "$status"
                break
                ;;
        esac
    done < "$event_pipe"
}
```

### local_driver_stop_session()
```bash
local_driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Kill tmux session
    tmux kill-session -t "$session_id" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    return 0
}
```

### local_driver_interrupt_session()
```bash
local_driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    
    # Send Ctrl+C
    tmux send-keys -t "$session_id" C-c
    
    return 0
}
```

## Session Monitoring Loop
```bash
local_driver_monitor_sessions() {
    local -a sessions=("$@")
    
    while [[ ${#sessions[@]} -gt 0 ]]; do
        local -a active_sessions=()
        
        for session_id in "${sessions[@]}"; do
            local state
            state=$(local_driver_get_session_state "$session_id" | jq -r '.state')
            
            case "$state" in
                complete)
                    handle_session_complete "$session_id"
                    ;;
                error)
                    handle_session_error "$session_id"
                    ;;
                waiting)
                    handle_session_waiting "$session_id"
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
                *)
                    active_sessions+=("$session_id")
                    ;;
            esac
        done
        
        sessions=("${active_sessions[@]}")
        sleep 2
    done
}
```

## Testing
- Launch session, verify tmux created
- Send message, verify delivered
- Detect AskUserQuestion event
- Detect session completion
- Interrupt and stop work correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sessions launch in tmux with stream-json
- [ ] Events parsed from NDJSON stream
- [ ] AskUserQuestion detected
- [ ] Messages sent via tmux send-keys
- [ ] Session cleanup works
- [ ] Works without ntm installed","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.53945729-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:40:33.683297256-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9s7y","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.79309431-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-9xjc","title":"Phase 6: Error Handling and Recovery","description":"Phase 6: Error Handling and Recovery

Overview
--------
Robust error handling, retry strategies, checkpointing, and recovery
mechanisms to handle the many failure modes in a multi-session system.

Why Critical?
-------------
Many things can fail:
- Network timeouts
- API rate limits (GitHub, Anthropic)
- Session crashes
- User interrupts (Ctrl+C)
- Disk full
- Authentication expiry

Without proper handling, partial work is lost.

Components
----------

6.1 Error Categories
   Classify errors for appropriate handling:
   - Transient: retry with backoff
   - Rate Limit: backoff + rotate
   - Auth: alert user, pause
   - Session: restart, preserve state
   - User: graceful handling
   - Fatal: abort with clear message

6.2 Retry with Exponential Backoff
   For transient failures:
   - Base delay with exponential growth
   - Jitter to prevent thundering herd
   - Max attempts limit
   - Configurable per operation

6.3 Checkpointing
   Periodic state saves for recovery:
   - After each significant operation
   - Includes all necessary context
   - Atomic writes prevent corruption
   - Configurable interval

6.4 Resume from Checkpoint
   Continue interrupted reviews:
   - Load checkpoint state
   - Identify completed/pending work
   - Skip already-done repos
   - Restore question queue

6.5 Graceful Degradation
   When advanced features unavailable:
   - ntm missing: use local driver
   - gum missing: use ANSI fallback
   - Tests fail: continue without push
   - Rate limited: reduce parallelism

6.6 Signal Handling
   Proper cleanup on interrupts:
   - SIGINT (Ctrl+C): save state, exit
   - SIGTERM: save state, exit
   - SIGHUP: continue in background?
   - Set SYNC_INTERRUPTED flag

Exit Criteria
-------------
- All error categories handled
- Retry logic works correctly
- Checkpoints saved periodically
- Resume continues from checkpoint
- Graceful degradation works
- Signals handled cleanly

Estimated Effort
----------------
~300 lines of Bash","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:59.930455089-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:42:59.930455089-05:00"}

-{"id":"bd-koxf","title":"Define review plan artifact schema and validation","description":"# Task: Define Review Plan Artifact Schema and Validation

## Purpose
Define the machine-readable JSON schema that Claude must produce for each review session. This artifact is the contract between the Plan phase and Apply phase.

## Background: Why a Structured Artifact?
- Apply phase needs to know what to do
- Can't scrape agent transcripts reliably
- Enables resume after interruption
- Provides audit trail
- Enables metrics without parsing text

## File Location
```
{worktree}/.ru/review-plan.json
```

## Schema (Version 1)

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "repo": "owner/repo",
  "worktree_path": "/home/user/.local/state/ru/worktrees/.../owner_repo",
  
  "items": [
    {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "title": "Authentication fails on Windows",
      "priority": "high",
      "decision": "fix",
      "notes": "Root cause: path separator in auth.py:234",
      "risk_level": "low",
      "files_changed": ["src/auth.py"],
      "lines_changed": 5
    },
    {
      "type": "pr",
      "number": 15,
      "title": "Add Redis caching",
      "priority": "normal",
      "decision": "skip",
      "notes": "Out of scope - adds external dependency",
      "risk_level": "n/a"
    }
  ],
  
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q1",
      "prompt": "Should I refactor all path handling or just fix this case?",
      "options": [
        {"label": "Quick fix", "description": "Fix only auth.py (5 lines)"},
        {"label": "Full refactor", "description": "Modernize all paths (45 lines)"},
        {"label": "Skip", "description": "Not a priority"}
      ],
      "recommended": "Quick fix",
      "answered": true,
      "answer": "Quick fix",
      "answered_at": "2025-01-04T10:35:00Z"
    }
  ],
  
  "git": {
    "branch": "ru/review/20250104-103000-12345/owner-repo",
    "base_ref": "main",
    "commits": [
      {
        "sha": "abc123def456",
        "subject": "Fix Windows path handling in auth.py",
        "files": ["src/auth.py"],
        "insertions": 3,
        "deletions": 2
      }
    ],
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  },
  
  "gh_actions": [
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "body": "Fixed in commit abc123. The issue was path separators on Windows..."
    },
    {
      "op": "close",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "reason": "completed"
    },
    {
      "op": "label",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]
    },
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "pr#15",
      "body": "Thank you for the suggestion. After review, this is out of scope..."
    }
  ],
  
  "metadata": {
    "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
    "completed_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
    "duration_seconds": 900,
    "context_usage_percent": 45,
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4",
    "driver": "local"
  }
}
```

## Field Definitions

### Top Level
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| schema_version | int | yes | Always 1 for this version |
| run_id | string | yes | Unique review run identifier |
| repo | string | yes | owner/repo format |
| worktree_path | string | yes | Absolute path to worktree |
| items | array | yes | Work items reviewed |
| questions | array | no | Questions asked/answered |
| git | object | no | Git state (may be empty if no changes) |
| gh_actions | array | no | GitHub actions to perform in Apply |
| metadata | object | yes | Session metadata |

### Item Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| type | "issue"\|"pr" | yes | Work item type |
| number | int | yes | Issue/PR number |
| title | string | yes | Item title |
| priority | string | yes | high/normal/low |
| decision | string | yes | fix/skip/needs-info/closed |
| notes | string | no | Explanation of decision |
| risk_level | string | no | low/medium/high/n/a |
| files_changed | array | no | Files modified for this item |
| lines_changed | int | no | Net lines changed |

### Question Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| id | string | yes | Unique question ID |
| prompt | string | yes | Question text |
| options | array | no | Available options |
| recommended | string | no | Agent's recommendation |
| answered | bool | yes | Whether answered |
| answer | string | no | Selected answer (if answered) |
| answered_at | string | no | ISO timestamp |

### gh_action Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| op | string | yes | comment/close/label/merge |
| target | string | yes | issue#N or pr#N |
| body | string | no | Comment body (for comment op) |
| reason | string | no | Close reason (for close op) |
| labels | array | no | Labels to add (for label op) |

## Validation Function
```bash
validate_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    # Must exist
    [[ ! -f "$plan_file" ]] && { echo "Plan file not found"; return 1; }
    
    # Must be valid JSON
    if ! jq empty "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Invalid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Required top-level fields
    if ! jq -e '.schema_version and .repo and .items' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Missing required fields: schema_version, repo, or items"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Schema version check
    local version
    version=$(jq -r '.schema_version' "$plan_file")
    if [[ "$version" != "1" ]]; then
        echo "Unsupported schema version: $version"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Items must have required fields
    if ! jq -e '.items | all(.type and .number and .decision)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Items missing required fields: type, number, or decision"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate decision values
    local invalid_decisions
    invalid_decisions=$(jq -r '.items[] | select(.decision | IN("fix","skip","needs-info","closed") | not) | .decision' "$plan_file")
    if [[ -n "$invalid_decisions" ]]; then
        echo "Invalid decision values: $invalid_decisions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate gh_actions if present
    if jq -e '.gh_actions' "$plan_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! jq -e '.gh_actions | all(.op and .target)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
            echo "gh_actions missing required fields: op or target"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "Valid"
    return 0
}
```

## Plan Summary
```bash
summarize_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    jq -r '
        "Repository: \(.repo)",
        "Items reviewed: \(.items | length)",
        "  - Fixed: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "fix")] | length)",
        "  - Skipped: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "skip")] | length)",
        "  - Needs info: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "needs-info")] | length)",
        "Commits: \(.git.commits // [] | length)",
        "Tests: \(if .git.tests.ok then "PASS" else "FAIL/NOT RUN" end)",
        "gh_actions pending: \(.gh_actions // [] | length)"
    ' "$plan_file"
}
```

## Testing
- Validate correct plan passes
- Validate missing fields rejected
- Validate invalid decisions rejected
- Parse all field types correctly
- Handle optional fields gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema documented with all fields
- [ ] Validation function catches all errors
- [ ] Example plans provided for testing
- [ ] Summary function works
- [ ] Optional fields handled correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:01.268595285-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:37:37.427168446-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:37:37.427168446-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}

 {"id":"bd-ldyv","title":"Implement quality gates framework (tests/lint before push)","description":"Task: Implement Quality Gates Framework

Purpose
-------
Run tests and lint before allowing push, ensuring code quality and
preventing broken commits from reaching the repository.

Background: Defense in Depth
----------------------------
AI can make mistakes. Quality gates catch:
- Syntax errors
- Test failures
- Lint violations
- Security issues (secrets in code)
- Build failures

Gate Types
----------

1. Test Gate
   Run project test suite
   Command: auto-detect or configured
   Failure: blocks push

2. Lint Gate
   Run linters/formatters
   Command: configured per-repo
   Failure: blocks push

3. Secret Scan Gate
   Check for exposed secrets
   Tool: gitleaks or regex fallback
   Failure: blocks push with warning

4. Build Gate (optional)
   Verify project builds
   Command: configured
   Failure: blocks push

Implementation
--------------

run_quality_gates()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local plan_file="$2"
  
  # Load per-repo policy
  local repo_id=$(jq -r .repo "$plan_file")
  local policy="$RU_CONFIG_DIR/review-policies.d/${repo_id//\//_}.conf"
  [[ -f "$policy" ]] && source "$policy"
  
  # Run lint if configured
  if [[ -n "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD" ]]; then
    log_step "Running lint: $REVIEW_LINT_CMD"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$REVIEW_LINT_CMD"); then
      log_error "Lint failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  # Run tests
  run_test_gate "$wt_path" || return 1
  
  # Secret scanning
  run_secret_scan "$wt_path" || return 2  # 2 = needs review
  
  log_success "Quality gates passed"
  return 0

run_test_gate()
  local wt_path="$1"
  local test_cmd="${REVIEW_TEST_CMD:-}"
  
  # Auto-detect if not configured
  if [[ -z "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    if [[ -f "$wt_path/Makefile" ]] && grep -q "^test:" "$wt_path/Makefile"; then
      test_cmd="make test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/package.json" ]]; then
      test_cmd="npm test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/Cargo.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="cargo test"
    elif [[ -f "$wt_path/setup.py" || -f "$wt_path/pyproject.toml" ]]; then
      test_cmd="pytest"
    fi
  fi
  
  if [[ -n "$test_cmd" ]]; then
    log_step "Running tests: $test_cmd"
    if \! (cd "$wt_path" && eval "$test_cmd"); then
      log_error "Tests failed"
      return 1
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

run_secret_scan()
  local wt_path="$1"
  
  if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
    log_step "Scanning for secrets with gitleaks"
    if \! gitleaks detect --source "$wt_path" --no-git; then
      log_error "Secrets detected in changes"
      return 1
    fi
  else
    # Regex fallback
    if git -C "$wt_path" diff HEAD~1..HEAD | \
       grep -qiE "password\s*=|api.?key\s*=|secret\s*=|token\s*="; then
      log_warn "Potential secrets detected"
      return 2  # Needs human review
    fi
  fi
  
  return 0

Policy Configuration
--------------------
~/.config/ru/review-policies.d/owner_repo.conf:

REVIEW_TEST_CMD="make test"
REVIEW_LINT_CMD="npm run lint"
REVIEW_REQUIRE_TESTS=true
REVIEW_SECRET_SCAN=true

Gate Results in Plan
--------------------
Update plan with gate results:
{
  "git": {
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  }
}

Testing
-------
- Verify auto-detection works
- Verify configured commands run
- Verify failures block push
- Verify secret scan catches patterns
- Test with missing tools

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Test gate runs before push
- [ ] Lint gate runs if configured
- [ ] Secret scan warns on patterns
- [ ] Failures block push with message
- [ ] Per-repo policies work
- [ ] Auto-detection works for common projects","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:44.686321504-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:44.686321504-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:14.491001528-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ldyv","depends_on_id":"bd-cutq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:10:25.431274346-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mcvj","title":"Phase 7: Security, Metrics, and Testing","description":"Phase 7: Security, Metrics, and Testing

Overview
--------
Security safeguards, analytics collection, and comprehensive testing
to ensure the review system is safe, measurable, and reliable.

Components
----------

7.1 Security: Command Validation
   Prevent dangerous commands:
   - Blocked list: sudo, rm -rf, eval
   - gh mutation blocking in Plan mode
   - Approval required for risky ops
   - Pre-exec hook validation

7.2 Security: Secret Scanning
   Detect secrets before push:
   - gitleaks integration
   - Regex fallback patterns
   - Block push on detection
   - Human review option

7.3 Metrics Collection
   Track review analytics:
   - Reviews per period
   - Issues resolved
   - Questions asked/answered
   - Time per repo
   - Decision breakdown

7.4 Decision Tracking
   Learn from past decisions:
   - Record decision patterns
   - Suggest based on history
   - Per-repo preferences
   - JSONL format for analysis

7.5 Analytics Dashboard
   Visualize review effectiveness:
   - Overview stats
   - Decision breakdown chart
   - Top repos by activity
   - Efficiency trend

7.6 Unit Tests
   Test individual functions:
   - GraphQL batching
   - Priority scoring
   - JSON parsing
   - State management

7.7 Integration Tests
   Test full workflows:
   - Discovery to apply
   - Question handling
   - Error recovery
   - Resume functionality

7.8 Test Fixtures
   Mock data for testing:
   - Claude stream examples
   - GraphQL responses
   - Review plan examples
   - Error scenarios

Exit Criteria
-------------
- Security controls prevent dangerous ops
- Metrics collected accurately
- Analytics dashboard works
- Unit test coverage adequate
- Integration tests pass
- Fixtures enable reliable testing

Estimated Effort
